name: reply
title: Discord Reply
description: Reply on the messaging channel. Pass chat_id from the inbound message. Optionally pass reply_to, files, embeds, and components (buttons, selects, etc).

chat_id.description: Channel ID where the message will be sent

reply_to.description: Message ID to thread under. Use message_id from the inbound <channel> block, or an id from fetch.

files.description: Absolute file paths to attach (images, logs, etc). Max 10 files, 25MB each.

embeds.description: Discord embed objects. Fields: title, description, color (int), fields [{name, value, inline}], footer {text}, timestamp.

components.description: Discord message components. Use Action Rows containing Buttons, Select Menus, etc. See Discord Components V2 docs.

name: react
title: Reaction
description: Add an emoji reaction to a message. Unicode emoji work directly; custom emoji need the <:name:id> form.

message_id.description: ID of the message to react to

emoji.description: Unicode emoji (e.g. "👍") or custom emoji in <:name:id> format

name: edit_message
title: Edit Message
description: Edit a message the bot previously sent. Supports text, embeds, and components.

message_id.description: ID of the bot message to edit

name: download_attachment
title: Download Attachment
description: Download attachments from a message to the local inbox. Use after fetch shows a message has attachments (marked with +Natt). Returns file paths ready to Read.

message_id.description: ID of the message with attachments

name: fetch
title: Fetch
description: Fetch recent messages from a channel. Returns oldest-first with message IDs. The platform's search API isn't exposed to bots, so this is the only way to look back.

channel.description: Channel name label (e.g. "main", "general") as configured in channelsConfig

name: schedule_status
title: Schedule Status
description: Show all configured schedules, their next fire time, and whether they are currently running.

name: trigger_schedule
title: Trigger Schedule
description: Manually trigger a named schedule immediately, ignoring time/day constraints.

name: schedule_control
title: Schedule Control
description: Defer or skip a schedule. Use "defer" to suppress for N minutes (default 30), or "skip_today" to suppress for the rest of the day.

minutes.description: Defer duration in minutes (default 30, only for defer action)

name: activate_channel_bridge
title: Activate Channel Bridge
description: Activate or deactivate the channel bridge. When active, inbound messages trigger typing indicators, emoji reactions, and auto-forwarding of transcript output to Discord. When inactive, only direct MCP tool calls (reply, fetch) work.

active.description: true to activate, false to deactivate

name: reload_config
title: Reload Config
description: Reload config from disk and re-register all schedules, webhooks, and event rules without restarting.

name: memory
title: Memory Cycle
description: Run memory operations: cycle2/sleep (promote+dedup), flush, rebuild, prune, cycle1, backfill, status, remember (store fact).

maxDays.description: Age threshold in days for the `prune` action. Unclassified entries older than this are deleted. Default 30, minimum 1. Ignored by other actions.

name: recall
title: Recall
description: Past context from the memory store. `query`: string or array (parallel fan-out). Async; merged answer auto-pushed. External web → `search`, codebase → `explore`.

query.description: Natural language query or array (parallel fan-out).

cwd.description: Optional workspace hint. Absolute path; `~` and forward slashes supported.

wait.description: Async by default. Pass `wait:true` to block inline.

name: explore
title: Explore
description: Internal codebase search. `query`: string or array (parallel fan-out). `cwd` is authoritative search root (no silent fan-out). Async; merged answer auto-pushed. Past context → `recall`, external web → `search`.

query.description: Natural language query or array (parallel fan-out).

cwd.description: Authoritative search root. Absolute path; `~` and forward slashes supported. Omit → launch workspace. Target plugin tree via `cwd: "~/.claude/..."`. No silent fan-out.

wait.description: Async by default. Pass `wait:true` to block inline.

name: search
title: Search
description: External web search, URL scrape, GitHub code/issues/repos. `query`: string or array (parallel fan-out); URL → scrape, `owner/repo` → GitHub. Async; merged answer auto-pushed. Past context → `recall`, codebase → `explore`.

query.description: Natural language query or array (parallel fan-out).

wait.description: Async by default. Pass `wait:true` to block inline.

name: create_session
title: Create Session
description: Create external AI session with tool access. Auto-injects context. Use preset: full/readonly/mcp. Use agent: Worker/Reviewer.

provider.description: openai, anthropic, gemini, groq, openrouter, xai, copilot, ollama, lmstudio, local

model.description: Provider-specific model id (e.g. "gpt-5", "claude-opus-4", "gemini-2.5-pro"). Must be valid for the chosen provider.

systemPrompt.description: Optional system prompt prepended to the session. When omitted, the plugin injects the default Pool B/C prefix based on role/preset.

preset.description: Tool permission preset: `full` grants read+write+shell, `readonly` restricts to read-only tools, `mcp` exposes only MCP-bridged tools.

files.description: Optional virtual files seeded into the session's working context (path+content pairs). The agent can read these immediately without fs access.

name: list_sessions
title: List Sessions
description: List active orchestrator sessions.

name: close_session
title: Close Session
description: Close an orchestrator session.

sessionId.description: Id of the session to close (as returned by `create_session` or `list_sessions`). Plants a tombstone and aborts any in-flight work; re-closing the same id is a no-op.

name: list_models
title: List Models
description: List available models from all providers.

name: bridge
title: Bridge to External Model
description: Delegate one turn of work to an external agent by role. Role maps to a preset via user-workflow.json (e.g. worker→OPUS XHIGH, reviewer→GPT5.4). Use this to hand off code, research, debug, review, or test work — the lead does not do these directly.

prompt.description: The task instruction for the agent.

role.description: Agent role as defined in user-workflow.json. Default roles: worker, researcher, reviewer, debugger, tester. Users may customize — check user-workflow.json for the actual set.

preset.description: Advanced: explicit preset name (bypass role mapping).

context.description: Extra context appended to the prompt.

file.description: Read prompt from a file path.

name: read
title: Read
description: Read a file with cat -n line numbering, size cap, optional offset/limit.

name: edit
title: Edit
description: Replace one unique occurrence of `old_string` with `new_string`. Must match exactly once.

name: write
title: Write
description: Create or overwrite a file.

name: bash
title: Bash
description: Execute a shell command. Destructive patterns (rm -rf /, force-push, format) are blocked.

name: grep
title: Grep
description: ripgrep content search. `pattern` / `glob` accept string or array (OR-joined). Output modes: `files_with_matches` (default), `content`, `count`.

-n.description: Show line numbers (content mode, default true).

name: glob
title: Glob
description: File path search via `rg --files`. `pattern` accepts string or array (OR-joined).

path.description: Base dir. Default: cwd. Capped at 100.

name: multi_edit
title: Multi Edit
description: Apply ordered replacements to ONE file. Any match failure aborts the batch (no partial writes). `replace_all:true` drops uniqueness check.

name: multi_read
title: Multi Read
description: Read several files in parallel. Returns `### <path>` sections; per-file errors inline.

name: list
title: List Directory
description: Directory listing with metadata (name, type, size, mtime). Faster + more useful than `bash ls` because it returns parseable rows and respects head_limit. For pure path-pattern search use `glob` (rg --files backend); for content search use `grep`. Defaults to depth 1 — increase for recursive walk.

path.description: Directory to list. Defaults to cwd. Supports `~` expansion.

depth.description: Recursion depth (1 = direct children only, max 10). Default 1.

type.description: Filter by entry type. Default any.

name: tree
title: Directory Tree
description: ASCII directory tree visualization. Quick way to grasp project shape before diving in. For metadata (size/mtime) use `list`; for content search use `grep`. Defaults to depth 3.

path.description: Root directory. Defaults to cwd. Supports `~` expansion.

name: find_files
title: Find Files (metadata filter)
description: Find files by metadata — name pattern, size range, modification time range, type. Complements `glob` (path patterns) and `grep` (content). Useful for "files modified in the last 24h", "files larger than 10MB", etc.

min_size.description: Minimum size in bytes (file only). Optional.

max_size.description: Maximum size in bytes (file only). Optional.

name: head
title: Head N Lines
description: Read the first N lines of a file. Cleaner than `read` with offset:0+limit:N when you just want a quick peek at the top of a file. For middle-of-file ranges use `read` with offset/limit.

n.description: Number of lines to read from the top. Default 20.

name: tail
title: Tail N Lines
description: Read the last N lines of a file — typically used for log inspection. For full-file or specific ranges use `read`.

n.description: Number of lines from the bottom. Default 20.

name: wc
title: Word Count
description: Count lines, words, and bytes of a file. Faster than reading the whole file when you just need size metrics.

name: batch_edit
title: Batch Edit
description: Apply edits across multiple files. Per-entry failures reported as `FAIL <path>`; batch continues.

name: diff
title: Unified Diff
description:
Unified diff between two files (or two raw strings). Useful for explaining a change before applying it via `edit` / `multi_edit`. Output is standard `--- from
+++ to
@@ ... @@` format. Pass `from_text:true` and/or `to_text:true` to treat the value as inline text instead of a path.

from.description: Path of the "before" file, or raw text when `from_text:true`.

to.description: Path of the "after" file, or raw text when `to_text:true`.

from_text.description: Treat `from` as inline text. Default false.

to_text.description: Treat `to` as inline text. Default false.

context.description: Context lines around hunks. Default 3, clamp [0, 10].

name: lsp_definition
title: LSP Definition
description: Resolve a symbol to its canonical definition via the TypeScript language server. Pass `symbol` (the name to resolve) and `file` (a TS/JS/MJS file that contains or imports the symbol). Returns one or more `path:line:col` locations. Prefer this over `grep` for any symbol that appears in multiple files — the LSP picks THE definition, grep picks every textual match. Only TypeScript / JavaScript files are supported (.ts .tsx .js .jsx .mjs .cjs).

symbol.description: Symbol name to resolve (identifier, class, function, method).

file.description: Absolute or cwd-relative path to a file that contains or imports the symbol. The server anchors its semantic analysis from this document.

name: lsp_references
title: LSP References
description: List every call site / reference of a symbol across the workspace, using the TypeScript language server. Pass `symbol` and `file` (same contract as `lsp_definition`). Returns a newline-separated `path:line:col` listing, declaration included. Unlike grep, unrelated occurrences of the same name (different scopes, shadowed vars) are excluded.

symbol.description: Symbol name to look up references for.

file.description: Absolute or cwd-relative path to a file that contains the symbol. Used to anchor the LSP cursor.

name: lsp_hover
title: LSP Hover
description: Get hover info (type signature, JSDoc) for a symbol via the TypeScript language server. Faster than reading the file when you just need to know what a name resolves to. Pass `symbol` and `file` (same contract as `lsp_definition`). Returns the rendered hover content or "(no hover info)".

file.description: Absolute or cwd-relative path to a file that contains or imports the symbol.

name: lsp_symbols
title: LSP Document Symbols
description: Outline a TS/JS file: class / function / method / variable declarations with line numbers, hierarchical when the server supports it. Pass `file`. Good first pass before diving in — shows the shape of a file without reading its body.

file.description: Absolute or cwd-relative path to the TS/JS file to outline.
